do_not_use_when: You want an AI-provenance or authorship verdict (it produces a distance, not a call)
You want a morphology- or function-word-aware voice claim (use the English voice_distance for those)
Target and baseline are in different languages (cross-language comparison is meaningless)
Target is under 500 words (the length floor)

examples.description: Spanish voice distance vs a same-language baseline

required: --lang language code shared by target and corpus (provenance)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

purpose: A language-agnostic, parser-free stylometric distance between a target and a baseline corpus — character n-grams, punctuation profile, token-/sentence- length distributions, and script statistics. No spaCy, no English assumption; runs on any Unicode script with zero model dependencies. Honest about its ceiling: language-agnostic, NOT language-aware (no morphology, no function-word list), so it refuses morphology-dependent voice claims and refuses cross-language comparison (the --lang tag is provenance).
use_when: You want a voice distance for non-English prose, or any language without an English parser
You have a register-matched, same-language baseline corpus and a target in the same language
